title: Ensure IAM policy changes are monitored

input_row.title: Ensure IAM policy changes are monitored
input_row.description: Real-time monitoring of API calls can be achieved by directing CloudTrail Logs to CloudWatch Logs or an external Security Information and Event Management (SIEM) environment, and establishing corresponding metric filters and alarms. It is recommended that a metric filter and alarm be established for changes made to Identity and Access Management (IAM) policies.
input_row.rationale: CloudWatch is an AWS native service that allows you to observe and monitor resources and applications. CloudTrail logs can also be sent to an external Security Information and Event Management (SIEM) environment for monitoring and alerting. Monitoring changes to IAM policies will help ensure authentication and authorization controls remain intact.
input_row.audit: If you are using CloudTrail trails and CloudWatch, perform the following to ensure that there is at least one active multi-region CloudTrail trail with the prescribed metric filters and alarms configured: 1. Identify the log group name that is configured for use with the active multi-region CloudTrail trail: • List all CloudTrails: • aws cloudtrail describe-trails • Identify multi-region CloudTrail trails: Trails with "IsMultiRegionTrail" set to true • Note the value associated with "Name":<trail-name> • Note the <trail-log-group-name> within the value associated with "CloudWatchLogsLogGroupArn" o Example: arn:aws:logs:<region>:<account-id>:log- group:<trail-log-group-name>:* • Ensure the identified multi-region CloudTrail trail is active: • aws cloudtrail get-trail-status --name <trail-name> o Ensure IsLogging is set to TRUE • Ensure the identified multi-region CloudTrail trail captures all management events: • aws cloudtrail get-event-selectors --trail-name <trail-name> o Ensure there is at least one event selector for a trail with IncludeManagementEvents set to true and ReadWriteType set to All 2. Get a list of all associated metric filters for the <trail-log-group-name> captured in step 1: 3. aws logs describe-metric-filters --log-group-name <trail-log-group- name> 4. Ensure the output from the above command contains the following: 5. "filterPattern": "{($.eventName=DeleteGroupPolicy)||($.eventName=DeleteRolePolicy)||($.eventName=DeleteUserPolicy)||($.eventName=PutGroupPolicy)||($.eventName=PutRolePolicy)||($.eventName=PutUserPolicy)||($.eventName=CreatePolicy)||($.eventName=DeletePolicy)||($.eventName=CreatePolicyVersion)||($.eventName=DeletePolicyVersion)||($.eventName=AttachRolePolicy)||($.eventName=DetachRolePolicy)||($.eventName=AttachUserPolicy)||($.eventName=DetachUserPolicy)||($.eventName=AttachGroupPolicy)||($.eventName=DetachGroupPolicy)}" 6. Note the <iam-changes-metric> value associated with the filterPattern from step 3. 7. Get a list of CloudWatch alarms, and filter on the <iam-change-metric> captured in step 4: 8. aws cloudwatch describe-alarms --query 'MetricAlarms[?MetricName==<iam-changes-metric>]' 9. Note the AlarmActions value; this will provide the SNS topic ARN value. 10. Ensure there is at least one active subscriber to the SNS topic: 11. aws sns list-subscriptions-by-topic --topic-arn <sns-topic-arn> • At least one subscription should have "SubscriptionArn" with a valid AWS ARN. o Example of valid "SubscriptionArn": arn:aws:sns:<region>:<account- id>:<sns-topic-name>:<subscription-id>
input_row.remediation: If you are using CloudTrail trails and CloudWatch, perform the following steps to set up the metric filter, alarm, SNS topic, and subscription: 1. Create a metric filter based on the provided filter pattern that checks for IAM policy changes and the <trail-log-group-name> taken from audit step 1: 2. aws logs put-metric-filter --log-group-name `<trail-log-group-name>` --filter-name `<iam-changes-metric>` --metric-transformations metricName= `<iam-changes-metric>`,metricNamespace='CISBenchmark',metricValue=1 --filter-pattern '{($.eventName=DeleteGroupPolicy)||($.eventName=DeleteRolePolicy)||($.eventName=DeleteUserPolicy)||($.eventName=PutGroupPolicy)||($.eventName=PutRolePolicy)||($.eventName=PutUserPolicy)||($.eventName=CreatePolicy) ||($.eventName=DeletePolicy)||($.eventName=CreatePolicyVersion)||($.eventName=DeletePolicyVersion)||($.eventName=AttachRolePolicy)||($.eventName=DetachRolePolicy)||($.eventName=AttachUserPolicy)||($.eventName=DetachUserPolicy)||($.eventName=AttachGroupPolicy)||($.eventName=DetachGroupPolicy)}' Note: You can choose your own metricName and metricNamespace strings. Using the same metricNamespace for all Foundations Benchmark metrics will group them together. 3. Create an SNS topic that the alarm will notify: 4. aws sns create-topic --name <sns-topic-name> Note: You can execute this command once and then reuse the same topic for all monitoring alarms. Note: Capture the TopicArn that is displayed when creating the SNS topic in step 2. 5. Create an SNS subscription for the topic created in step 2: 6. aws sns subscribe --topic-arn <sns-topic-arn> --protocol <sns-protocol> --notification-endpoint <sns-subscription-endpoints> Note: You can execute this command once and then reuse the same subscription for all monitoring alarms. 7. Create an alarm that is associated with the CloudWatch Logs metric filter created in step 1 and the SNS topic created in step 2: 8. aws cloudwatch put-metric-alarm --alarm-name `<iam-changes-alarm>` --metric-name `<iam-changes-metric>` --statistic Sum --period 300 --threshold 1 --comparison-operator GreaterThanOrEqualToThreshold --evaluation-periods 1 --namespace 'CISBenchmark' --alarm-actions <sns-topic-arn>
input_row.impact: Monitoring these changes may result in a number of "false positives," especially in larger environments. This alert may require more tuning than others to eliminate some of those erroneous notifications.
input_row.default_value: By default, CloudTrail records IAM policy change events, but no CloudWatch metric filters or alarms are configured. This means changes (e.g., CreatePolicy, AttachRolePolicy, DeletePolicy) are logged but not actively monitored or alerted on unless you explicitly set up filters and alarms.
input_row.additional_information: Configuring a log metric filter and alarm on a multi-region (global) CloudTrail trail: • ensures that activities from all regions (both used and unused) are monitored • ensures that activities on all supported global services are monitored • ensures that all management events across all regions are monitored

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
- The audit can be fully validated using AWS APIs via the AWS CLI and boto3. The provided description and audit process includes specific commands that can be executed to gather the necessary data and verify compliance regarding CloudTrail and CloudWatch metrics and alarms for IAM policy changes. 

**PROGRAM NAME:** aws_cloudtrail_multi_region_iam_policy_changes_monitoring

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
1. **AWS API Call / boto3 SDK Methods:**
   - Use `boto3.client('cloudtrail')` to interact with CloudTrail.
   - Use `boto3.client('logs')` to check the metric filters in CloudWatch Logs.
   - Use `boto3.client('cloudwatch')` to verify the CloudWatch alarms.
   - Use `boto3.client('sns')` to check SNS topic subscriptions.

2. **AWS CLI Commands:**
   - List CloudTrails: `aws cloudtrail describe-trails`
   - Get Trail Status: `aws cloudtrail get-trail-status --name <trail-name>`
   - Get Event Selectors: `aws cloudtrail get-event-selectors --trail-name <trail-name>`
   - List Metric Filters: `aws logs describe-metric-filters --log-group-name <trail-log-group-name>`
   - Describe Alarms: `aws cloudwatch describe-alarms --query 'MetricAlarms[?MetricName==<iam-changes-metric>]'`
   - List Subscriptions: `aws sns list-subscriptions-by-topic --topic-arn <sns-topic-arn>`

3. **Specific Resources to Enumerate:**
   - Multi-region CloudTrail trails.
   - Log groups associated with these trails.
   - Metric filters established on these log groups.
   - CloudWatch alarms synced with the specified metric filters.
   - SNS topics and subscribed endpoints.

4. **Configuration Properties/Settings to Validate:**
   - Ensure multi-region CloudTrail is active and logging management events.
   - Validate metric filters following the specified IAM policy change pattern.
   - Confirm the presence of alarms linked to the above metric filters.
   - Ensure that at least one active subscriber exists for the SNS topic associated with the alarm.

5. **Clear Pass/Fail Criteria with Expected Values:**
   - **Pass:** At least one active multi-region CloudTrail that is logging events, successfully configured metric filter with an appropriate pattern for IAM policy changes, at least one alarm linked to that metric, and at least one subscriber to the SNS topic associated with the alarm.
   - **Fail:** Any of the above checks does not meet the conditions listed.

6. **Example Validation Process:**
   - For each multi-region CloudTrail trail, check if it's logging using `get-trail-status`. If `IsLogging` is `TRUE`, proceed to the next step.
   - Validate the metric filters exist using the log group name gathered from the CloudTrail.
   - Check if alarms are configured that correspond to the IAM policy change metric.

In summary, this automated approach will leverage AWS services via API calls and commands to validate the compliance control for monitoring IAM policy changes through the established CloudTrail, CloudWatch, and SNS configurations.
